blocks:
Ayah

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُو۟لِىٓ أَجْنِحَةٍ مَّثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ ۚ يَزِيدُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱)
All praise belongs to Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth, maker of the angels [His] messengers, possessing wings, two, three or four [of them]. He adds to the creation whatever He wishes. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (1)

Key Points

Fāṭir (فاطر) means innovative creation, and ajniḥah (اجنحِة), derived from janāḥ (جناح), means wing and is a metaphor for power. In the Persian language, we also say: "So-and-so's wings are burned or broken," which is a metaphor for lacking power.

Messages

1- Praise must be based on worth and merit. (If the Quran states: «الحمدللّه», it is because He is Fāṭir (Creator), Jā'il (Appointer), and Qadīr (Powerful).)

2- Although Allah possesses power, He has established the system of creation based on causes and effects. «جاعل الملائكة رسلا»

3- The role and power of the angels are varied. «اولى اجنحة مثنى و ثلاث و رباع»

4- Allah's hand is open in creation, and the universe is in a state of development and expansion. «یزید فى الخلق ما یشاء»

5- The creation of the universe is a novel and innovative matter. «فاطر السموات و الارض»

Portrait of Angels in the Quran

The Holy Quran contains several points regarding angels, including:

  1. Angels are honored servants of Allah. «بل عباد مكرمون»(524)

  2. They are obedient to Allah. «لا یسبقونه بالقول و هم بامره یعملون»(525) «لا یعصون اللّه ما امرهم و یفعلون ما یُؤمرون»(526)

  3. The management and distribution of affairs are entrusted to them. «فالمدبّرات أمراً»(527) «فالمقسّمات أمراً»(528)

  4. They monitor human words and speech. «ما یلفظ من قولٍ الا لدیه رقیبٌ عتیدٌ»(529)

  5. They are the scribes of deeds. «و رسلنا لدیهم یكتبون»(530)

  6. They are bearers of good news to fighters in war. «بجنود لم تروها»(531)

  7. They are bearers of good news regarding having children. «انا نبشرك بغلام اسمه یحیى»(532)

  8. They are bearers of good news to believers at the moment of death. «الاّ تخافوا ولا تحزنوا»(533)

  9. They are agents for the punishment of criminals. «لمّا جاءت رسلنا لوطا سى ء بهم»(534)

  10. They are protectors of humans. «یُرسل علیكم حفظة»(535)

  11. They pray for believers. «و یستغفرون للذین آمنوا»(536) «و یستغفرون لمن فى الارض»(537)

  12. They intercede. «و كم من ملك فى السموات لا تغنى شفاعتهم شیئا الاّ من بعد ان یأذن اللّه لمن یشاء و یرضى»(538)

  13. They curse the disbelievers. «أولئك علیهم لعنة اللّه والملائكة والنّاس أجمعین»(539)

  14. They are helpers on the battlefronts. «یُمدّكم ربّكم بثلاثة آلاف من الملائكة منزلین»(540) «بلى ان تصبروا و تتّقوا... یمددكم ربّكم بخمسة آلاف»(541)

  15. They punish criminals at the moment of death. «توفّتهم الملائكة یضربون وجوههم و أدبارهم»(542)

  16. They welcome the people of Paradise. «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم»(543)

  17. They are responsible for the torment of Hell. «علیها تسعة عشر»(544)

  18. They are responsible for seizing the soul. «توفّته رسلنا»(545)

  19. They possess ranks. «ما منّا الا له مقام معلوم»(546)

  20. They are responsible for the descent of revelation. «ینزّل الملائكة بالروح»(547)

  21. They sometimes appear in human form. «فتمثّل لها بشراً سویّا»(548)

  22. They do not tire of worship. «یسبّحون له بالیل والنهار و هم لا یسأمون»(549)

  23. They also have contact and conversation with people other than the Prophets. «انّما انا رسول ربّك لاهب لك غلاماً»(550)

  24. Some angels are chosen. «اللّه یصطفى من الملائكة رسلا و من الناس»(551)

  25. Belief in angels is necessary. «والمؤمنون كلّ آمن باللّه و ملائكته و كتبه و رسله»(552) «و مَن یكفر باللّه و ملائكته و كتبه و رسله و الیوم الآخر فقد ضلّ ضلالاً بعیدا»(553)

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Fāṭir. This Surah was revealed in Mecca and contains forty-five verses. Fāṭir (فاطر) is one of the attributes of Allah, meaning the Creator. The name of this Surah is taken from its first verse, which introduces Allah as «فاطر السموات و الارض» (Originator of the heavens and the earth). Like other Meccan Surahs, the main focus of its discussions revolves around the origin (of creation), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and the struggle against polytheism (shirk).

Ayah

مَّا يَفْتَحِ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا ۖ وَمَا يُمْسِكْ فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲)
Whatever mercy Allah unfolds for the people, no one can withhold it; and whatever He withholds no one can release it except Him, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (2)

Messages

1- In this world, all people benefit from divine mercy. «یفتح اللّه للناس من رحمة»

2- Divine mercy precedes His wrath. (The word «یفتح» [opens] comes before «یمسك» [withholds].)

3- Allah's giving and withholding are accompanied by wisdom. «یفتح - یمسك - الحكیم»

4- Divine blessings possess treasures, only a portion of which is opened to mankind. «یفتح - مرسل»

5- Expecting help from anyone other than Allah is futile; if He does not will it, no power is effective. «فلا ممسك - فلا مرسل»

6- The Will of Allah is inviolable. «ما یفتح... فلا ممسك لها و ما یمسك فلا مرسل»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ هَلْ مِنْ خَـٰلِقٍ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ (۳)
O mankind! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you! Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the sky and the earth? There is no god except Him. So where do you stray? (3)

Messages

1- We should invite everyone to reflect upon the divine blessings. «یا ایّها الناس»

2- The best way to know Allah is to pay attention to His blessings. «اذكروا نعمت اللّه»

3- Questioning is the key to awakening and warning. «هل من خالق...»

4- Both the beginning and creation of existence are from Him, as is its survival and continuation. «خالق - یرزقكم»

5- He is the Provider (Raziq), but He provides sustenance (rizq) through natural means. «یرزقكم من السماء و الارض»

6- Reproach has no meaning before a warning. «هل من خالق... فأنّى تُؤفكون»

7- Every path other than Tawhid (monotheism) is a deviation. «فأنّى تُؤفكون»

8- Beware lest we consume the sustenance of Allah and turn toward others besides Him. «یرزقكم - فأنّى تُؤفكون»

9- Human nature (fitrah) is inclined toward God; external factors become the causes of his deviation. «تُؤفكون»

Ayah

وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (٤)
If they impugn you, certainly [other] apostles were impugned before you, and all matters are returned to Allah. (4)

Messages

1- All prophets had opponents, and the denial of the truth is the permanent practice of the disbelievers. «كُذّبت رسل من قبلك»

2- History repeats itself. «یكذّبوك... كُذّبت رسل من قبلك»

3- The disbelievers are not concerned with the person; they oppose the path and the goal. Whoever raises the call of truth will be subjected to denial. «یكذّبوك - من قبلك»

4- History is the best source of solace in the face of difficulties. «كُذّبت رسل من قبلك»

5- The denial by the people does not harm the truthfulness of the revelation; those denied before you were Our very own Messengers. «كُذّبت رسل»

6- Attention to the Resurrection makes man steadfast against bitter events. «یكذبوك... الى اللّه ترجع الامور»

7- Opponents of the truth should know that their obstinacies will not be forgotten or abandoned. «ترجع الامور»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ (٥)
O mankind! Allah’s promise is indeed true. So do not let the life of the world deceive you, nor let the Deceiver deceive you concerning Allah. (5)

Key Points

Gharūr («غَرور») refers to someone who is extremely deceptive (a great deceiver). Based on the following Ayah, the intended meaning here is Satan. Perhaps the meaning of being deceived concerning Allah in the phrase "لا یغرّنّكم باللّه" (let not the great deceiver deceive you concerning Allah) is that Satan makes man complacent regarding Allah's pardon and forgiveness, or that he compels him to repeat sins through distortion, innovation (bid'ah), and negligence concerning divine laws and decrees. The beginning of this Surah was about Tawhid (monotheism), the previous Ayah was about prophethood, and this Ayah is about the Resurrection (Ma'ad). A similar Ayah is also found in Surah Luqman, Ayah 33.

Messages

1- Some groups are deceived by material and worldly life, but others are not fooled by the glitter of the world; rather, the deceptive Satan misguides them. «لا یغرنّكم باللّه الغرور»

2- For the purpose of education (or moral training), warnings must be repeated. «فلا تغرنّكم - ولا یغرنكّم»

3- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the best deterrent against the deception of the world. «وعداللّه حقّ»

4- Divine promises are true, but the promises of Satan are vain and deceptive. «وعداللّه حقّ»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ فَٱتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا ۚ إِنَّمَا يَدْعُوا۟ حِزْبَهُۥ لِيَكُونُوا۟ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (٦)
Satan is indeed your enemy, so treat him as an enemy. He only invites his confederates so that they may be among the inmates of the Blaze. (6)

Key Points

The word Sa'īr (سعیر), meaning blazing fire, is one of the names of Hell. The history of Satan's enmity towards humanity is ancient: He expelled our father Adam from Paradise. «كما اخرج أبویك»(554) He is an enemy who is not seen. «انّه یراكم هو و قبیله من حیث لا ترونهم»(555) He is a sworn enemy. ««فبعزّتك...»(556) He attacks man from every direction. ««من بین أیدیهم و من خلفهم...»(557) His means for misleading people are the promise of poverty and the command to indecency. «الشیطان یعدكم الفقر و یأمركم بالفحشاء»(558) He is not satisfied except by making people inhabitants of the Fire. «یدعوا حزبه لیكونوا من اصحاب السعیر» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "If Satan is an enemy, then why the heedlessness towards him?"(559)

554) Al-A'raf, 27. 555) Al-A'raf, 27. 556) Sad, 82. 557) Al-A'raf, 17. 558) Al-Baqarah, 268. 559) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- If we do not take Satan's enmity seriously, we will be deceived. «عدوّ فاتّخذوه عدوّا»

2- We must be aware of the enemy's plans and organizations. «الشیطان... یدعوا حزبه»

3- Satan seeks help to mislead people; what do we do to invite others to the truth? «الشیطان... یدعوا حزبه»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ (۷)
There is a severe punishment for the faithless; but for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, there will be forgiveness and a great reward. (7)

Messages

1- There is an accountability (a reckoning) involved in obeying or neglecting the divine commands. «كفروا لهم عذاب... آمنوا... لهم مغفرة»

2- To maintain the spirit of fear and hope, the promise (of reward) and the threat (of punishment) must be presented together. «عذاب شدید - اجر كبیر»

3- Considering the consequences and paying attention to the final outcome is effective in moral development. «لهم عذاب - لهم مغفرة»

4- Disbelief (kufr) is sufficient for incurring punishment, «كفروا لهم عذاب» but attaining reward requires good deeds in addition to faith. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات لهم مغفرة»

5- Unless sins are forgiven, the path to entering Paradise and receiving the reward is not paved. «لهم مغفرة و اجر كبیر»

6- First, the completion of the proof (ittmam al-hujjah), then the threat of retribution. (The preceding verses constituted the completion of the proof, and this verse declares the punishment and the reward.)

Ayah

أَفَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُۥ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ فَرَءَاهُ حَسَنًا ۖ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَٰتٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ (۸)
Is someone the evil of whose conduct is presented as decorous to him, so he regards it as good.... Indeed Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and guides whomever He wishes. So do not fret yourself to death regretting for them. Indeed Allah knows best what they do. (8)

Key Points

The one who makes a person's bad behavior appear beautiful and offers flattery instead of criticism is Satan, because in another Ayah we read: «زیّن لهم الشیطان اعمالهم» (560) Leaving rhetorical/reproachful questions unanswered is a sign of threatening and humiliating the transgressors. To reproach the evildoers, Allah poses a question and leaves it unanswered. He says: Is the transgressor who sees his bad behavior as good... but He does not provide the answer to this sentence. Imam Kadhim علیه السلام said: "Seeing bad deeds as good is the prelude to conceit (self-admiration)." (561)

560) Anfal, 48. 561) Kafi, vol 2, p 313.

Messages

1- Love of beauty is part of human nature, to the extent that Satan also makes bad deeds appear beautiful to compel man to commit them. «سوء عمله فرءاه حسنا»

2- Considering bad deeds as good is the ground for misguidance. «زُیّن له... یُضلّ من یشاء»

3- Man first justifies the sin and then proceeds to commit it. (The beautification of bad deeds is the same as the justification of transgressions.) «زیّن له سوء عمله»

4- The believer is realistic, self-aware, and insightful, but the disbeliever is delusional, deceived, and conceited. (The previous Ayah spoke of disbelievers and believers, and this Ayah speaks of realism versus delusion.) «الّذین كفروا... زیّن له سوء عمله»

5- Good deeds and bad deeds must be measured by the Divine standard, not by the delusions of others. «سوء عمله فرءاه حسنا»

6- Misunderstanding and considering bad deeds as good prevents accepting advice, repentance, development, and growth. «زیّن له - یضل من یشاء»

7- Although guidance and misguidance are from Allah, they occur based on human actions. Disbelievers stubbornly choose the path of deviation, and Allah increases their misguidance. «یهدى - یضل - بما یصنعون»

8- The Prophet was eager and distressed for the guidance of the people. «لا تذهب نفسك»

9- Regret and sorrow must have limits. «فلا تذهب نفسك علیهم حسرات»

10- Paying attention to the fact that the actions of the transgressors are under the supervision of Allah reduces our sorrow. «فلا تذهب نفسك علیهم حسرات انّ اللّه علیم بما یصنعون»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَسُقْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلنُّشُورُ (۹)
It is Allah who sends the winds and they raise a cloud; then We drive it toward a dead land and with it revive the earth after its death. Likewise will be the resurrection [of the dead]. (9)

Key Points

Just as, by the will of Allah, the movement of the cloud and the wind and the pouring of the rain revive the dead earth and plants emerge from the soil, by another will and the occurrence of a great earthquake, the dead will rise from the soil and be revived. This new spring after the leaf-fall is proof of the existence of the Resurrection (562)

(562) Mathnawi of Mawlawi (Rumi).

Messages

1- The blowing of the winds, the movement of the clouds, and the pouring of the rains are not accidental; they are by Divine will. «اللّه... ارسل الریاح فتثیر... فسقناه»

2- Program, order, and management govern existence. «ارسل... فتثیر... فسقناه... فاحیینا»

3- To understand the unseen (Ghayb), use the witnessed (Shahud). «كذلك النشور»

Ayah

مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ ٱلْكَلِمُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ وَٱلْعَمَلُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُ يَرْفَعُهُۥ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَمَكْرُ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ (۱٠)
Whoever seeks honour [should know that] honour entirely belongs to Allah. To Him ascends the good word, and He elevates righteous conduct; as for those who devise evil schemes, there is a severe punishment for them, and their plotting shall come to naught. (10)

Key Points

"الكلم الطیّب" (The good word) is the testimony to Tawhid (Oneness of God), Prophethood (Risalah), and Guardianship (Wilayah). In narrations, its instances include invocations (Adhkar) such as "لا اله الاّ اللّه" (There is no god but Allah) and "سبحان اللّه" (Glory be to Allah), and others. "یبور" (will perish/become vain) derives from Bawār and Bā'ir, meaning great stagnation/dullness (of trade) and destruction (هلاك). Other meanings can also be stated for this noble Ayah: 1. The good word ascends toward God, but righteous action grants it elevation and rank. 2. The good word itself ascends and also elevates good deeds. Disbelievers seek honor (‘Izzah) either through attachment to material things, "و اتّخذوا من دون اللّه آلهة لیكونوا لهم عزّا" (And they have taken gods besides Allah that they might be for them a source of might) (563), or they seek it through dependence on others, "أیبتغون عندهم العزّة" (Do they seek honor with them?) (564). Yet, honor belongs only to Allah. "فللّه العزّة جمیعاً" (Then to Allah belongs all honor).

(563) Maryam, 81. (564) Nisa, 139.

Messages

  1. True honor (‘Izzah) is with Allah, not with people. "فللّه العزّة جمیعا"

  2. True honor is achieved under the shade of faith and righteous action. "فللّه العزّة جمیعا... الكلم الطیّب و العمل الصالح"

  3. There is a close relationship between belief and action, and they influence one another. "العمل الصالح یرفعه"

  4. The path of the believers, who seek honor through faith and righteous action, is the path of growth and perfection. "یصعد - یرفع" (ascends - elevates). The path of others, who seek honor through deceit and trickery, leads to annihilation and destruction. "یبور"

  5. No one achieves honor through trickery and sin. "یبور"

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا ۚ وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِن مُّعَمَّرٍ وَلَا يُنقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِۦٓ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (۱۱)
Allah created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then He made you mates. No female conceives or delivers except with His knowledge, and no elderly person advances in years, nor is anything diminished of his life, but it is [recorded] in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah. (11)

Key Points

The word mu'ammar (مُعَمَّر) is used for those who possess a long life. ‘Umr (عُمر) derives from ‘imrān (development/cultivation) and ābādī (prosperity/habitation). Since a human being has the ability to cultivate and develop during the days of his life, the duration of his existence is called ‘umr. In narrations, charity (sadaqah), maintaining kinship ties (silat al-rahm), and kindness to parents are introduced as causes for a long life, while severing kinship ties and disrespecting parents are introduced as causes for a short life. (565)

565) Mizan al-Hikmah, (‘Umr).

Messages

1- Pay attention to the origin of your creation so that you may realize the power of Allah and avoid falling into heedlessness, arrogance, and pride. «خلقكم من تراب ثم من نطفة»

2- Both Adam (عليه السلام) was created from dust and existing human beings originate from a sperm-drop whose source is dust. «خلقكم من تراب»

3- The primary elements of human existence are dust and water. «تراب، نطفه»

4- The creation of pairs (spouses) for humanity is among the Divine favors. «جعلكم ازواجاً»

5- Allah is aware of all the conditions of a being, even before its birth and appearance. «و ما تحمل من اُنثى ولا تضع الاّ بعلمه»

6- Man has a determined lifespan, but for certain reasons, it is sometimes reduced. «لاینقص من عمره»

7- Every moment of human life is accounted for. «فى كتاب»

8- Creating man from dust and a sperm-drop, and knowing the moments of human life, is easy for Allah. «على اللّه یسیر»

Ayah

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْبَحْرَانِ هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ سَآئِغٌ شَرَابُهُۥ وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ ۖ وَمِن كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا ۖ وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۱۲)
Not alike are the two seas: this one sweet and agreeable, pleasant to drink, and that one briny and bitter, and from each you eat fresh meat and obtain ornaments, which you wear. And you see the ships plowing through them, that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may give thanks. (12)

Key Points

‘Adhb means sweet, furāt means pleasant/gentle, sā'igh means palatable/wholesome, and mawākhir is the plural of ma'kharah, meaning cleaver/splitter (of waves). The lives of individuals are sometimes sweet and sometimes bitter. Sometimes accompanied by fame and sometimes by anonymity. Sometimes accompanied by wealth, sometimes by poverty. Sometimes with health and sometimes with sickness. However, if a person is a good hunter, diver, and captain, he can be successful in all contradictory conditions. "و من كلّ تأكلون... و تستخرجون" (And from both you eat... and extract) Utilizing fish, extracting ornamental materials, and navigation are blessings that accompany human effort. Otherwise, the blessings of the sea are numerous, including: the formation of clouds from sea vapor, air purification, and the nurturing of various aquatic creatures.

Messages

1- Speak of the sweet things first. (Although most seas have salty and bitter water, sweet water is mentioned first in the Quranic statement.) "هذا عذب - هذا ملح" (This is sweet - This is salty).

2- Raw consumption (vegetarianism/raw foodism) and the prohibition of meat are not accepted in Islam. "تأكلون لحماً" (You eat meat).

3- The sea is the source of human food, clothing, and transport. "تأكلون - تلبسونها - ترى الفلك" (You eat - You wear them - You see the ships).

4- In nutrition, one must seek fresh meat. "لحماً طریّاً" (Fresh meat).

5- Man has an innate inclination toward beauty, and benefiting from adornment is accepted in Islam. "تستخرجون حلیة تلبسونها" (You extract ornaments which you wear).

6- Divine blessings are His favor. We have no claim upon Him. "من فضله" (From His bounty/favor).

7- Divine sustenance (Rizq) and favor are attained through man's own effort and striving. "لتبتغوا من فضله" (That you may seek of His bounty).

8- Attention to blessings is the ground for the flourishing of the spirit of gratitude. "تشكرون" (That you may be grateful).

Ayah

يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ (۱۳)
He makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night, and He has disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs all sovereignty. As for those whom you invoke besides Him, they do not control so much as the husk of a date stone. (13)

Key Points

Night and day gradually yield their place to one another, preventing the harmful effects of the abrupt transition from darkness to light and vice versa.

Messages

  1. The shortening and lengthening of night and day, or the gradual transition of each into the other, is not accidental. «یولج»

  2. Nature is subservient to Allah. «سخّر الشمس...»

  3. The movements of the moon and the sun have a schedule and an end. «یجرى لاجل مُسمّى»

  4. Strengthen your monotheism by paying attention to the creation that is orderly, purposeful, and scheduled. «ذالكم اللّه ربّكم»

  5. In the method of propagation (Da'wah), first explain the face of truth, then compare it with the face of falsehood. «ذالكم اللّه - والّذین تدعون من دونه»

  6. Supplication and request must be directed toward a superior authority; idols are neither superior to you nor do they possess ownership of benefit or harm. «ما یملكون»

  7. Where is man going? He abandons the absolute Divine power «له الملك» and turns toward beings that possess no power whatsoever. «ما یملكون من قطمیر»

  8. In the method of propagation, use examples that all people, in all times and places, can understand. «قطمیر»

Ayah

إِن تَدْعُوهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ دُعَآءَكُمْ وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا۟ مَا ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ (۱٤)
If you invoke them they will not hear your invocation, and even if they heard they cannot respond to you, and on the Day of Resurrection they will forswear your polytheism, and none can inform you like the One who is all-aware. (14)

Key Points

A sect of Muslims (Wahhabis) uses this Ayah to support their belief that seeking intercession (Tawassul) through the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the Imams علیهم السلام is impermissible. They are unaware that these verses concern seeking intercession through idols, not the Divine Saints (Awliya Allah), with whom Allah speaks even after their death. In numerous verses of the Quran, Allah has sent greetings (Salam) to the Prophets, including: «سلام على ابراهیم»(566), «سلام على موسى و هارون»(567). Truly, if they do not hear the greeting of Allah, then what meaning does the greeting have?! It seems that the life in Barzakh (the intermediate realm) enjoyed by the martyrs is not accepted by this group! It is mentioned in many narrations that the Prophet of Islam spoke with the dead. For instance, at the well of Badr, He spoke to the slain disbelievers who had been thrown into the well. When He was questioned about speaking to the dead, He replied: «شما از آنان شنواتر نیستید» (You are not more hearing than they are).(568)

566) Saffat, 109. 567) Saffat, 120. 568) For more information, refer to the author's book on Ma'ad (Resurrection).

Messages

1- Do not withhold any argument in order to eliminate polytheism (Shirk). «لایملكون من قطمیر» (Previous Ayah) «لایسمعوا دعاء كم» They do not hear your speech or your cries. «ما استجابوا لكم» Even if they were to hear, they would not respond. «یكفرون بشرككم» On the Day of Judgment, even the worshipped objects will detest the polytheists.

2- Humanity reaches a point where it abandons the call of the All-Hearing, Responsive God, «ادعونى استجب لكم» (Call upon Me; I will respond to you), and turns instead to deaf, blind, and useless inanimate objects. «ان تدعوهم لایسمعوا... و ما استجابوا»

3- False deities will disavow the polytheists on the Day of Judgment. «یكفرون بشرككم» And they will say: You did not worship us, but rather you were slaves to your own imaginations and desires. «و قال شركاؤهم ما كنتم ایّانا تعبدون»(569)

4- Polytheism is beneficial neither in this world, «ما استجابوا», nor in the Hereafter. «یكفرون بشرككم»

5- Take important news from the experts. «و لا یُنبّئك مثل خبیر»

6- News of the Day of Judgment cannot be received except through revelation. «و یوم القیامة... لا یُنبّئك مثل خبیر»569) Yunus, 28.

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ أَنتُمُ ٱلْفُقَرَآءُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (۱٥)
O mankind! You are the ones who stand in need of Allah, and Allah—He is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable. (15)

Key Points

Allah's invitation to people is not a sign of His need for them. Just as if we tell people to build their houses facing the sun, this invitation is not a sign of the sun's need for us, but rather a sign of our need for light. «انتم الفقراء الى اللّه» One of the ways to combat arrogance and pride is to invite man to the threshold of Allah so that he may realize his own weakness, ignorance, poverty, and need. «انتم الفقراء»

Messages

1- No one is independent of Allah. «یا ایّها النّاس»

2- All beings are poor (in need), but because man makes claims and rebels, he must be restrained. «یا ایّها النّاس»

3- We accept the role of intermediaries, means, and causes, but all means and causes are also dependent on Him for their effectiveness. «هو الغنّى»

4- The true, absolute, and perfect Rich One is only Him. «واللّه هوالغنّى»

5- Usually, the rich are not beloved and are targets of insults, envy, competition, and theft, but Allah is the Rich, the Praiseworthy (Al-Ghani Al-Hamid). «واللّه هوالغنّى الحمید»

6- Allah uses His richness to fulfill the needs and benefit His creatures, and is therefore worthy of praise. «الغنى الحمید»

Ayahs

إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (۱٦)
If He wishes, He will take you away, and bring about a new creation; (16)
وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ (۱۷)
and that is not a hard thing for Allah. (17)

Key Points

In Ayah 133 of Surah Al-An'am, we read a verse similar to Ayah 16, which stated: «و ربّك الغنّى ذو الرّحمة اِن یشأ یذهبكم و یستخلف من بعدكم ما یشاء كما أنشأكم من ذرّیة قوم آخرین» (And your Lord is the Self-Sufficient, the Possessor of Mercy. If He wills, He can do away with you and substitute after you whom He wills, just as He produced you from the descendants of another people.) Your Lord is Self-Sufficient and Possessor of Mercy. If He wills, He takes you away and substitutes after you whomever He wills, just as He made you successors to those who came before.

Messages

1- If man considers the ground beneath his feet weak and dangerous, he proceeds more cautiously. «ان یشأ یذهبكم»

2- Among the signs of man's poverty is the possibility of his annihilation. «انتم الفقراء... یذهبكم»

3- Stating Divine power is a means of inviting people and a threat to the rebellious. «ان یشأ یذهبكم»

4- Divine power is equal in taking away and bringing forth creation. «یذهبكم - یأت»

5- The Hand of Allah is open in creation, and His power knows no deadlock. «یذهبكم و یأت بخلق جدید»

6- A manager must prevent the arrogance and monopolistic tendencies of his subordinates. «یذهبكم و یأت بخلق جدید»

7- We must take Divine power seriously. «و ما ذلك على اللّه بعزیز»

Ayah

وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَإِن تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ إِلَىٰ حِمْلِهَا لَا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَىْءٌ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰٓ ۗ إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۚ وَمَن تَزَكَّىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَتَزَكَّىٰ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱۸)
No bearer shall bear another’s burden, and should someone heavily burdened call [another] to carry it, nothing of it will be carried [by anyone] even if he should be a near relative. You can only warn those who fear their Lord in secret and maintain the prayer. Whoever purifies himself, purifies only for his own sake, and to Allah is the return. (18)

Key Points

The word wizr (وِزر) means heaviness and burden. Muthqalah (مُثْقَلة) means heavily burdened. Himl (حِمل) refers to a burden carried on the back, and haml (حَمل) refers to a burden carried in the belly (pregnancy).(570) We read in the Quran that a group, in order to deceive and mislead others, says: «اتّبعوا سبیلنا و لنحمل خطایاكم»(571) (Follow our path, and we will surely bear your sins). This Ayah provides the answer to them.

Question: Can we, based on this Ayah—that on the Day of Judgment, everyone bears his own burden and no one is harmed by another's burden—say that we should ignore sinners because the burden of their sin is on themselves and does not concern us?

Answer: This Ayah is not a justification for silence in the face of evil deeds (munkarat), because silence itself is a sin upon the silent individuals. Our duty is to enjoin the good and forbid the evil (Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy anil Munkar), but if they do not listen, their sin is not upon us.

Although everyone is solely responsible for his own actions, if someone establishes a wrong path or tradition (sunnah) in society, then in addition to those who deviate, the one who established that tradition is also responsible, because the guide to deviation and sin is a partner in that deviation and sin.

570) Tafsir Namuneh, quoting Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- Based on justice, everyone must bear his own burden. «لا تزر وازرة...»

2- We should not blame our sins on ancestors, friends, or the environment. «و لا تزر وازرة»

3- The reckoning and accountability of every person on the Day of Judgment is separate. «لا تزر وازرة»

4- Sin is a burden. Wizr - Muthqalah.

5- On the Day of Judgment, kinship ties have no effect on a person's destiny, and relatives will not lift any burden from a person. «و لو كان ذا قربى»

6- Reverence (khashyah) and prayer give a person the capacity for acceptance. «انّما تنذر الّذین یخشون...»

7- Purification (tazkiyah) is achieved through piety (taqwa) and prayer. Yakhshawna - Aqamū al-Ṣalāta - Tazakkā.

8- The purification of man is for his own benefit. «لنفسه»

9- You may not receive the benefit of purification in this world, but you will certainly receive it in the Hereafter. «الى اللّه المصیر»

10- Existence is purposeful and possesses an evolutionary movement. «الى اللّه المصیر»

Ayahs

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ (۱۹)
The blind one and the seer are not equal, (19)
وَلَا ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتُ وَلَا ٱلنُّورُ (۲٠)
nor darkness and light; (20)

Messages

1- Comparing virtues and vices, and perfections and deficiencies, is one of the best methods of teaching and education. «و ما یستوى الاعمى...»

2- The path of truth is only one (the word «النّور» is used in the singular form), but the paths of deviation are many. «الظلمات»

Ayahs

وَلَا ٱلظِّلُّ وَلَا ٱلْحَرُورُ (۲۱)
nor shade and torrid heat; (21)
وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَحْيَآءُ وَلَا ٱلْأَمْوَٰتُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسْمِعُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ بِمُسْمِعٍ مَّن فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ (۲۲)
nor are the living equal to the dead. Indeed Allah makes whomever He wishes to hear, and you cannot make those who are in the graves hear you. (22)
إِنْ أَنتَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ (۲۳)
You are but a warner. (23)

Key Points

The word "ظِلّ" (ẓill) means shade, and the word "حَرُور" (ḥarūr) means hot, scorching wind. In these verses, the believer (mu'min) and the disbeliever (kāfir) are likened to four things. The result of these four comparisons and analogies is that the believer is not equal to the disbeliever in terms of character and destiny. The believer is likened to the seeing, and the disbeliever to the blind. The believer to light, and the disbeliever to darkness. The believer to soothing shade, and the disbeliever to scorching, hot wind. The believer to the living, and the disbeliever to the dead. The believer is oriented towards growth and movement, because he possesses an eye that sees the truth, light, a pure soul, and a living heart. However, the disbeliever is unwilling to see the truth, does not accept it due to hardness of heart, and does not move on the path of truth because of the darkness of ignorance, prejudice, and rigidity.

Messages

1- Believers are people with living hearts who enjoy true life. Faith grants life to the individual and society, while disbelief is the cause of death for the individual and society. «ما یستوى الاحیاء و لا الاموات»

2- The mission of the Prophet is to warn, not to compel. «ما انتَ بمسمع مَن فى القبور»

3- The condition for the effectiveness of propagation (da'wah) is the readiness of the people; otherwise, the Prophet's propagation will also be ineffective. «ما انتَ بمسمع مَن فى القبور»

Ayah

إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۚ وَإِن مِّنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ (۲٤)
Indeed We have sent you with the truth as a bearer of good news and as a warner; and there is not a nation but a warner has passed in it. (24)

Messages

1- Allah is the Truth, and He has established the educational system through the prophets upon the basis of the Truth. «ارسلناك بالحقّ»

2- Fear and hope are effective when placed side by side. «بشیراً و نذیراً»

3- Although the duty of the prophets is to give good tidings and warn, due to the heedlessness of the people, the warnings of the prophets have been greater. «اِن من امّة الاّ خلا فیها نذیر»

4- The earth has never been devoid of a Divine Proof. «الاّ خلا فیها نذیر»

Ayahs

وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَبِٱلزُّبُرِ وَبِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُنِيرِ (۲٥)
If they impugn you, those before them have impugned [likewise]: their apostles brought them manifest proofs, [holy] writs, and illuminating scriptures. (25)
ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ (۲٦)
Then I seized the faithless. So how was My rebuttal! (26)

Key Points

"Zubur" (زُبُر) is the plural of "Zabūr" (زبور), meaning books that possess a kind of prominence or distinction.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the difficulties faced by others is a source of consolation for man. «ان یكذّبوك فقد كذّب»

2- The Prophets carried with them the best tools for guidance. «البیّنات - الزبر - الكتاب»

3- The denial of the Prophets was intentional, based on obstinacy, and occurred despite knowledge and awareness of the content of their call. «كذّب الّذین... جائتهم رسلهم بالبینات و بالزبر و بالكتاب المنیر»

4- Allah's wrath follows the completion of the argument (proof). «جائتهم رسلهم... ثم اخذتُ»

5- Stating Allah's wrath and power in punishing the disbelievers is a source of consolation for the believers and followers of the truth. «ثم اخذت»

6- Allah's wrath sometimes occurs in this world. «ثمّ أخذتُ الّذین كفروا» (Lest the disbelievers think they are abandoned or neglected.)

7- Disbelief is the cause of destruction. «اخذت الّذین كفروا»

8- A display of power is necessary. The punishment of the disbelievers was terrifying and astonishing. «فكیف كان نكیر»

9- Take heed from the fate of disbelieving societies. «فكیف كان نكیر»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ ثَمَرَٰتٍ مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهَا ۚ وَمِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ جُدَدٌۢ بِيضٌ وَحُمْرٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهَا وَغَرَابِيبُ سُودٌ (۲۷)
Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky, with which We produce fruits of diverse hues. And in the mountains are stripes, white and red, of diverse hues, and [others] pitch black? (27)

Key Points

Judad (جُدَد) is the plural of judda (جُدّه) and jadda (جادّه), meaning path or way. Bīḍ (بیض) is the plural of abyaḍ (أبیض), meaning white. Ḥumr (حُمْر) is the plural of aḥmar (أحمر), meaning red. And sūd (سود) is the plural of aswad (أسود), meaning black. Gharābīb (غرابیب) means intensely dark black. Perhaps the reason Arabs call the crow ghurāb is because its color is black. Although these verses mention three colors of the mountains—white, red, and black—the phrase mukhtalifan alwānuhā (مختلفاً الوانها) indicates that the mountains are not limited to these three colors.

Messages

1 - Awaken consciences through questioning. (Alam tara - اَلَم تَرَ)

2 - The address to the Prophet is, in reality, an address to all people. (Alam tara inna Allāh - اَلَم تَرَ انّ اللّه)

3 - Allah has established the cosmic order based on cause and effect. The cause of plant growth is water. (Fa akhrajnā bihi - فاخرجنا به)

4 - Allah creates colorful fruits from colorless water and uniformly colored earth. (Thamarātin mukhtalifan alwānuhā - ثمرات مختلفاً الوانها)

5 - The colorful lines and streaks in the mountains are not accidental. (Judadun bīḍun wa ḥumrun - wa gharābību sūd - جُدد بیض و حمر - غرابیب سود)

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱلدَّوَآبِّ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ كَذَٰلِكَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى ٱللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ٱلْعُلَمَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ غَفُورٌ (۲۸)
And of humans and beasts and cattle there are likewise diverse hues. Only those of Allah’s servants having knowledge fear Him. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-forgiving. (28)

Key Points

An'ām (أنعام) is the plural of Na'am (نَعَم), meaning camel, but it is also used for cattle and sheep. Although the word Dawābb (دوابّ), which means moving creatures, includes them, their names are mentioned separately due to their importance and the intense human need for these three animals. Khashyah (خشیت) is fear accompanied by reverence and stemming from knowledge and awareness. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "A scholar ('ālim) is one whose actions confirm his words; otherwise, he is not a scholar."(572) This Ayah states that knowledge leads to Divine Khashyah, and Ayah 28 of the same Surah states that Divine Khashyah leads to accepting admonition from the Prophets. Therefore, we conclude that knowledge must lead to accepting warnings. Thus, if we see that scientists do not accept admonition while common people accept warnings, we must say that the knowledge discussed in the Quran is different from the modern term. Rather, the intended meaning of knowledge is the realization of truth, possessing spiritual light (nūrāniyyah), insight (baṣīrah), and wisdom (ḥikmah). Impious scientists are considered ignorant (jāhil) in the mirror of the Quran; ignorant of the greatness of the Lord, ignorant of the mission and duty in this world, ignorant of the purpose of creation, ignorant of destiny and the Day of Judgment. Near the shrine of Imam Reza عليه السلام, I met Allamah Tabataba'i قدس سره and said: In the early years of my studies, whenever I started praying, I felt a state of weeping and humility (khushū'), but now that I have become educated, I am heedless in prayer. So, what does the Ayah "انّما یخشى اللّه من عباده العلماء" (Only those who have knowledge among His servants fear Allah) mean? He replied: The knowledge you possess is merely a set of information and memorized facts. If it were true knowledge, your servitude ('ubūdiyyah) would have increased.

571) عنكبوت، 12. 572) كافى، ج 1، ص 36.

Messages

1- The variation in the colors of humans and animals is one of the signs of Allah's power and greatness. «و من النّاس... مختلف الوانه»

2- In physical and material matters, humans are ranked alongside other creatures. «و من النّاس و الدّوابّ و الانعام»

3- Knowledge and awareness of the secrets of existence are the foundation for attaining Divine Khashyah. «انّما یخشى اللّه... العلماء»

4- Impious scientists have not attained true knowledge. «انّما یخشى اللّه من عباده العلماء»

5- There are many people devoted to worship, but only the scholars attain the station of Khashyah. «انّما یخشى اللّه من عباده العلماء»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتَـٰبَ ٱللَّهِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَأَنفَقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً يَرْجُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً لَّن تَبُورَ (۲۹)
Indeed those who recite the Book of Allah and maintain the prayer, and spend secretly and openly out of what We have provided them, expect a commerce that will never go bankrupt, (29)

Key Points

The previous Ayah discussed that only the scholars possess the fear (reverence) of Allah. It seems this Ayah describes the characteristics of true scholars: they have familiarity with the Quran, they establish prayer, and their charity (infaq) is sincere because they give both openly and secretly. If they were hypocrites, they would only give openly.

Tabūr (تبور) is derived from bawār (بوار), meaning severe stagnation or slump in trade. Since this leads to ruin and destruction, bawār is also used to mean perdition or annihilation.

In the previous Ayah, "He fears Allah" (یخشى اللّه) was mentioned, and in this Ayah, "they hope" (یرجون) is mentioned. Indeed, the men of God are balanced between fear (khawf) and hope (rajā).(573)

In the Quran, prayer and the Quran have been mentioned together repeatedly: "یتلون كتاب اللّه و اقاموا الصلاة" (574) They recite the Book of Allah and establish prayer. "یمسّكون بالكتاب و اقاموا الصلاة" (575) They hold fast to the heavenly Book and establish prayer. "اتل ما اُوحى الیك من الكتاب و اقم الصلاة" (576) Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer.

Advantages of Trade with Allah

1- Whatever capital we possess (health, knowledge, lifespan, honor, wealth, etc.) belongs to Him, so we should sell it back to Him. Indeed, if a child received land, materials, and money from his father and built a house, and then people came to buy the house, one of whom was the father himself, reason and conscience dictate that he should sell the house to his father, because the child himself and all his possessions are from Him, and selling to a stranger is not honorable.

2- Allah buys at the price of eternal Paradise, but whatever others buy results in loss and damage, because they buy cheaply and it is fleeting.

3- Allah accepts little, "فمَن یَعمل مثقال ذرّة" (Whoever does an atom's weight of good), but others do not accept little.

4- Allah, out of forgiveness and overlooking, conceals the defect of the commodity and buys it despite its shortcomings. In the supplication after prayer, we read: O Allah! If there is any flaw or deficiency in the bowing (rukūʿ) and prostration (sujūd) of my prayer, overlook it and accept my prayer. In the supplication of the month of Rajab, we read: "خاب الوافدون على غیرك و خسر المتعرّضون الاّ لك" (577) meaning, those who came to the door of other than You have failed, and those who turned to others have suffered loss.

573) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan. 574) Fatir, 29. 575) Al-A'raf, 170. 576) Al-Ankabut, 45. 577) Mafatih al-Jinan.

Messages

1- Recitation of the Quran (although recommended/supererogatory) is mentioned before the obligatory duties. "یتلون... اقاموا... انفقوا"

2- Thought and culture are prerequisites for action. "یتلون كتاب اللّه - اقاموا الصلاة"

3- For one who lovingly accepts the doctrine, their prayer and charity are certain. (The recitation of the Book is in the imperfect tense, indicating continuity of action and love for recitation, "یتلون," but prayer and charity are in the perfect tense, indicating the certainty of their performance.) "اقاموا - انفقوا"

4- Prayer must be accompanied by attending to the deprived. "اقاموا - انفقوا"

5- Human possessions are a Divine gift. "رزقناهم"

6- If we pay attention that our possessions are from Allah, we will not be stingy in giving charity. "انفقوا مما رزقناهم"

7- A portion of what is given should be spent in charity; the rest is for yourselves. "مما رزقناهم" (The word min (مِن) means 'some' or 'a portion of').

8- The object of charity is not only wealth, but people should also be helped with knowledge, honor, and power. "ممّا رزقناهم"

9- Charity should be both secret and open. (In secret charity, a person's sincerity grows, and in open charity, people are encouraged.) "سرّاً و علانیة" Of course, secret charity is superior, which is why it is mentioned before open charity. "سرّاً و علانیة"

10- Hope for salvation must be accompanied by thought, action, and charity; otherwise, hope without work is merely an illusion. "یتلون, اقاموا, انفقوا, یرجون"

11- Even with knowledge and the fear of Allah (which was in the previous Ayah), and with reciting the Quran, establishing prayer, and helping the deprived, do not consider yourselves deserving; only be hopeful. "یرجون"

12- In trade with Allah, there is not even one percent loss. "تجارة لن تبور"

13- Do not imagine that your wealth will be depleted by giving charity. "تجارة لن تبور"

14- In Islamic culture, the world is a market, man is the seller, Divine blessings are the capital, and the choice of customer rests with man. He can trade with Allah, or He can choose other than Allah.

Ayah

لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (۳٠)
so that He may pay them their full reward and enhance them out of His bounty. Indeed He is all-forgiving, all-appreciative. (30)

Key Points

In all the words of this Ayah, divine grace and favor shine forth: The reward is complete. "لیوفّیهم" (that He may pay them in full). Even though the capital and the success (Tawfiq) for the action come from Him, He still names it a reward. "اجورهم" (their rewards). The increase of favors and rewards from Allah. "یزید من فضله" (and increase them from His bounty).

Messages

1- Working to receive divine reward does not harm sincerity (Ikhlas). "لیوفّیهم اُجورهم"

2- Allah both gives the wage and gives extra. "لیوفّیهم اُجورهم و یزیدهم"

3- The complete reward stems from Allah being Shukoor (Most Appreciative). "لیوفّیهم... شكور"

4- Allah appreciates efforts and covers up shortcomings; we too should be grateful for the services of others and overlook their slips. "انّه غفور شكور"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ لَخَبِيرٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (۳۱)
That which We have revealed to you of the Book is the truth, confirming what was [revealed] before it. Indeed Allah is all-aware, all-seeing about His servants. (31)

Messages

1- The Qur’an contains no false speech, superstition, exaggeration, or illogical statements. «هوالحقّ»

2- The measure of truthfulness for every speech and book is the Qur’an. «هوالحقّ»

3- The heavenly Books confirm one another. «مصدّقاً»

4- Allah is aware both of humanity’s need for revelation and of the Prophet’s worthiness to receive revelation. (Al-Khabīr is aware of the inner reality, and Al-Baṣīr is aware of the outer reality.) «بعباده خبیر بصیر»

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱصْطَفَيْنَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ۖ فَمِنْهُمْ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ وَمِنْهُمْ سَابِقٌۢ بِٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (۳۲)
Then We made those whom We chose from Our servants heirs to the Book. Yet some of them are those who wrong themselves, and some of them are average, and some of them are those who take the lead in all the good works by Allah’s will. That is the greatest grace [of Allah]! (32)

Key Points

Regarding this noble Ayah, what I have chosen from among the multiple interpretations, and which is also consistent with the narrations (Riwāyāt), is that Allah says: We gave the Qur'an as an inheritance to the chosen ones among Our servants after the Prophet, who are the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) from the progeny of Lady Fatimah (علیها السلام). The reason We gave the sciences of the Qur'an to the chosen ones is that some servants are unjust (ظالم), some are moderate (مقتصد), and some—who, according to the narrations, are the Ahl al-Bayt (the Prophet's Household)—take precedence in all good deeds. Only this third group is chosen, not everyone. In numerous narrations, we read that the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) said: "The chosen servants of God who are the inheritors of the Book are us, the Ahl al-Bayt of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله)." (578)

(578) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Qur'an alone is not sufficient; it requires a protector and an executor. «اورثنا الكتاب»

2- The one who has the right to choose and grant religious responsibilities is the one who possesses deep knowledge of the condition of His servants. «انّ اللّه بعباده لخبیر بصیر - ثمّ اورثنا الكتاب الّذین اصطفینا»

3- The reason for being deprived of the lights of the Qur'an is ourselves. «منهم ظالم - و منهم مقتصد»

4- The condition for receiving the inheritance of the Qur'an is precedence in all good deeds, not just performing some of them. «بالخیرات»

5- Allah's choice is wise. Those who inherit the sciences of the Qur'an are those who take precedence in good deeds. «سابق بالخیرات»

6- Injustice (wrongdoing) originates from the person himself, «ظالم لنفسه» (wronging himself), but the success of taking precedence in good deeds is by the permission of Allah. «سابق بالخیرات باذن اللّه »

7- Inheriting the Qur'an is a great Divine favor. «ذلك هوالفضل الكبیر»

Ayah

جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ (۳۳)
Gardens of Eden, which they will enter, adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearl, and their garments therein will be of silk. (33)

Key Points

| The word "أساور" (asāwir), the plural of "سِوار" (siwār), is the Arabized form of the Persian word "دستواره" (dastwārah) and is among the Persian words that entered the Arabic language. (579)

579) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The multiplicity and diversity of blessings is a value. "جنّات" (Gardens)

2- We should not neglect the element of encouragement. "جنّات عدن - یحلّون" (Gardens of Eden - they shall be adorned)

3- The reward for temporary deprivation is eternal enjoyment. (If gold and silk were forbidden to men during the few days of this world, they will benefit from them in Paradise.) "یحلّون... من ذهب..." (They shall be adorned... with gold...)

4- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. (Gold and pearls relate to the adornment of the material body.) "اساور من ذهب" (bracelets of gold)

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا ٱلْحَزَنَ ۖ إِنَّ رَبَّنَا لَغَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (۳٤)
They will say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has removed all grief from us. Indeed Our Lord is all-forgiving, all-appreciative, (34)

Messages

1- The motto of the people of Paradise is the praise of Allah. «و قالوا الحمد للّه»

2- In the atmosphere of Paradise, there is no sorrow or grief. «اذهب عنّا الحزن»

3- In Paradise, alongside material blessings (mentioned in the previous Ayah), spiritual tranquility is emphasized. «اذهب عنّا الحزن»

4- Paradise reaches the inhabitants of Paradise due to Allah's forgiveness and appreciation. «اِنّ ربّنا لغفور شكور»

5- Concealing shortcomings and appreciating worthy servants are among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «اِنّ ربّنا لغفور شكور»

6- The people of Paradise possess merits for which Allah is appreciative and grateful to them. «لغفور شكور»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ ٱلْمُقَامَةِ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ لَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا نَصَبٌ وَلَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا لُغُوبٌ (۳٥)
who has settled us in the everlasting abode by His grace. In it we are untouched by toil, and untouched by fatigue.’ (35)

Key Points

Naṣab means fatigue and hardship, and Lughūb means inability and weakness, and is sometimes used for depression. In developed countries and affluent families, comfort is found, but tranquility is rare. However, the inhabitants of Paradise are both in complete comfort and far removed from sorrows and confusion.

Messages

1- Paradise is eternal. «دار المقامة»

2- The inhabitants of Paradise consider the blessings to be from the favor of Allah, not from their own deeds. «من فضله»

3- Hardship and sorrow will not even touch the inhabitants of Paradise. «لا یمسّنا»

4- Excessive longevity in this world brings fatigue, but in Paradise, there is neither fatigue nor sorrow. «لا یمسّنا... نَصَب... لُغوب»

5- The comfort and tranquility of Paradise are certain. (The phrase «لا یمسنا» is repeated.)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يُقْضَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ فَيَمُوتُوا۟ وَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُم مِّنْ عَذَابِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى كُلَّ كَفُورٍ (۳٦)
As for the faithless, there is for them the fire of hell: they will neither be done away with so that they may die, nor shall its punishment be lightened for them. Thus do We requite every ingrate. (36)

Key Points

Alongside the promises of Paradise, the warnings of Hell are mentioned so that there may be a balance between fear and hope. The inhabitants of Hell have two requests. One time they say: «لیقضِ علینا ربّك» (580) (Let your Lord put an end to us) so that we may find relief. Allah says: «لایُقضى علیهم فیموتوا» (Death will not be decreed for them so that they may die). There is no death in Hell for the inhabitants to die and be saved from the torment. Another time they wish that at least a reduction (in punishment) be given to them: «یخفّف عنّا یوماً من العذاب» (581) This Ayah states: «لایخفّف» (It will not be lightened). There is no reduction (in punishment).

580) Al-Zukhruf, 77. 581) Ghafir, 49.

Messages

1- Whoever progresses from being a disbeliever to being utterly ungrateful (kafūr) and closes all doors of felicity upon himself, all doors of salvation will also be closed upon him; there is neither death nor reduction (in punishment). «فیموتوا ولا یخفّف»

2- Disbelief (kufr) is a type of ingratitude for blessings. «الّذین كفروا... كلَّ كفور»

3- The individual is not important; the characteristic is important. «كلّ كفور»

Ayah

وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيهَا رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ۚ أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُم مَّا يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَن تَذَكَّرَ وَجَآءَكُمُ ٱلنَّذِيرُ ۖ فَذُوقُوا۟ فَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ (۳۷)
They shall cry therein for help: ‘Our Lord! Bring us out, so that we may act righteously—differently from what we used to do!’ ‘Did We not give you a lifelong enough that one who is heedful might take admonition? And [moreover] the warner had [also] come to you. Now taste [the consequence of your deeds], for the wrongdoers have no helper.’ (37)

Key Points

"صُراخ" (Ṣurākh) means a cry or wail that is accompanied by a request for help. In the two preceding verses, it was mentioned that the inhabitants of Paradise enjoy comfort and tranquility; in this verse, He says: the inhabitants of Hell are burning, wailing, and requesting help.

Messages

1- Not every request or wail should be heeded; the plea of a criminal is not acceptable. "ربّنا اخرجنا نعمل صالحا" (Our Lord, bring us out; we will do righteous deeds.) (Allah says: "ولو ردّوا لعادوا لما نُهوا عنه" If they were sent back, they would return to that which they were forbidden.)

2- What condemns a person to Hell is unrighteous deeds. "اخرجنا نعمل صالحا"

3- All the rebellious will one day fall into helplessness and supplication. "ربّنا اخرجنا..."

4- The Hereafter is not the place to compensate for what was lost. "اولم نعمركم..."

5- Many people in this world imagine that their deeds are righteous, but on the Day of Judgment, they realize they were not righteous, and thus they say: Our Lord! If we are saved from Hell, we will perform righteous deeds (not like the seemingly righteous deeds we did before). "غیر الّذى كنّا نعمل"

6- Allah has granted everyone an age sufficient for the proof (Hujjah) to be established against them. "اولم نعمّركم ما یتذكّر فیه"

7- Losing opportunities results in self-oppression. "اولم نعمّركم... فما للظالمین من نصیر"

8- The purpose of life is awareness, and a heedless person is as if he has not lived. "اولم نعمّركم ما یتذكّر"

9- Momentary remembrance is not effective, because every criminal remembers for a moment; remembrance must be continuous. "یتذكّر فیه"

10- Divine wrath does not occur unless a warner (Nadhīr) has come. "جاءكم النذیر فذوقوا"

11- Whoever disregards the warnings of the Prophets is a wrongdoer (Ẓālim). "جاءكم النذیر... فما للظالمین"

12- For the disbelievers, neither intercession, nor prayer, nor wailing is effective. "من نصیر"

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَـٰلِمُ غَيْبِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۳۸)
Indeed Allah is the knower of the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He knows well what is in the breasts. (38)

Key Points

We read in the previous verses that: Allah chose certain individuals to inherit the Qur'an, He takes some individuals to Paradise and some to the Fire (Hell); this verse states: because He knows everything and is aware of the hidden and the manifest, and of the inner and the outer.

Messages

1- Those who lost the opportunities of this world and did not perform righteous deeds, and who wail in the Hereafter, saying, "Save us from the Fire so that we may perform righteous deeds," are not truthful in their speech. «انه علیم بذات الصدور»

2- Belief that Allah knows everything is the best deterrent for man against wrongdoing. «انه علیم بذات الصدور»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ فَمَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا مَقْتًا ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ إِلَّا خَسَارًا (۳۹)
It is He who made you successors on the earth. So whoever is faithless, his unfaith is to his own detriment. The unfaith of the faithless does not increase them with their Lord [in anything] except disfavour, and their unfaith increases the faithless in nothing except loss. (39)

Key Points

The meaning of man being a Khalifah (vicegerent) in this verse is one of three things: the succession of nations by nations, the succession of the children of Adam to the humans who existed before Adam's creation, or the vicegerency of man on behalf of Allah.

Maqt (مَقت) means intense wrath, and it is used in the Quran regarding four things: 1. Disbelief (Kufr). «لا یزید الكافرین كفرهم... الاّ مقتاً» 2. Adultery/Fornication (Zina). «انّه كان فاحشة و مقتاً»(582) 3. Saying and not doing. «كَبُر مقتاً عنداللّه ان تقولوا مالا تفعلون»(583) 4. Illogical speech/Argumentation without authority. «یجادلون فى آیات اللّه بغیر سلطان اَتاهم كبر مقتاً عنداللّه»(584)

582) An-Nisa, 22. 583) As-Saff, 3. 584) Ghafir, 35.

Messages

  1. Man's vicegerency on Earth should be a cause for gratitude (Shukr), not disbelief (Kufr). «جعلكم خلائف... فمن كفر» (You too are transient, and others will succeed you; do not commit disbelief during these few days of worldly life.)
  2. Man's disbelief is detrimental only to himself and does not harm Allah. «فعلیه كفره»
  3. Disbelievers should not blame their disbelief on their ancestors or society. «من كفر فعلیه كفره»
  4. The dangers and effects of disbelief are not limited but expand constantly. «لا یزید الكافرین الاّ مقتا... الاّ خساراً»
  5. Disbelief, in itself, is a cause of loss, even if the disbeliever does not commit any corrupt act. «لا یزید الكافرین كفرهم الاّ خسارا»
  6. Stating the dangers of sin is a factor in deterring the sinner. «مقتاً - خساراً»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ شُرَكَآءَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَرُونِى مَاذَا خَلَقُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ أَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ كِتَـٰبًا فَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَتٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ بَلْ إِن يَعِدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ بَعْضُهُم بَعْضًا إِلَّا غُرُورًا (٤٠)
Say, ‘Tell me about your ‘partners’ whom you invoke besides Allah? Show me what [part] of the earth have they created. Have they any share in the heavens?’ Have We given them a scripture so that they stand on a manifest proof from it? No, the wrongdoers do not promise one another [anything] except delusion. (40)

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was commanded to engage in dialogue with the opponents. «قل»

2- Awaken consciences through questioning. «ءرأیتم - ارونى ماذا خلقوا»

3- Islam is the religion of logic. «ءرأیتم - ارونى - فهم على بیّنة»

4- The challenge (tahaddi) is not only found in the Book of Legislation (Kitab al-Tashri'), where He says: «فأتوا بسورة» (Then bring a Surah), but also regarding creation and the Book of Existence (Kitab al-Takwin). «ارونى ماذا خلقوا»

5- The polytheists have no logic whatsoever: neither rational logic «ماذا خلقوا» (what they have created) nor transmitted logic «ام آتیناهم كتاباً» (Or have We given them a book).

6- Wherever there is no logic or clear evidence, there is deception. «ان یعد... الاّ غرورا»

7- The polytheists are wrongdoers (zalimun). «یعد الظالمون» (The wrongdoers promise). The promise of the wrongdoers is deception, and their slogans are misleading. «الاّ غرورا»

8- Shirk (polytheism) is based on propaganda and deceptive promises. «ان یعد الظالمون بعضهم بعضا الاّ غرورا»

9- Deception can come from anyone. «بعضهم بعضا» (some of them to others). Sometimes weak and ordinary individuals mislead those higher up through flattery and sycophancy, and sometimes powerful individuals compel people to unquestioning obedience through threats, temptation, and false promises and slogans. «یعد الظالمون بعضهم بعضاً»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ أَن تَزُولَا ۚ وَلَئِن زَالَتَآ إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُمَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا (٤۱)
Indeed Allah sustains the heavens and the earth lest they should fall apart, and if they were to fall apart, there is none who can sustain them except Him. Indeed He is all-forbearing, all-forgiving. (41)

Key Points

There is a relationship between Allah's maintenance and preservation of the cosmic order and His forbearance (Hilm), just as there is a relationship between our beliefs and actions and the collapse of the cosmic order. This Ayah refers to the first case, and the Ayah concludes with the phrase, «حلیماً غفورا» (Forbearing, Forgiving). However, in Surah Maryam, Ayahs 88 to 90, we read: «و قالوا اتخذ الرحمن ولداً لقد جئتم شیئاً ادّا تكاد السموات یتفطرن منه و تنشقّ الارض و تخرّ الجبال هدّا» The polytheists said: Allah has taken a son. It is near that the heavens, the earth, and the mountains collapse and fall apart because of this vile statement. Allah maintains both the magnificent heavens and the earth «یمسك السموات و الارض» (He holds the heavens and the earth) and the small bird in the air «ما یمسكهن الاّ الرحمن» (None holds them except the Most Gracious) (585). We read in narrations: Allah preserves the heavens and the earth through His Awliya (saints/chosen ones), such that if they were not present, the cosmic order would fall apart (586).

585) ملک، 19. 586) تفسیر نورالثقلین.

Messages

1- The position of the heavens and the earth, and their movement and orbits, are by the Will of Allah, and it is He Who preserves them every moment. «انّ اللّه یمسك السموات و الارض»

2- Phenomena require Allah's power and grace both for their creation and for their continuation (survival). «انّ اللّه یمسك السموات...»

3- The order governing existence is from Allah, not a matter of chance. «انّ اللّه یمسك السموات...»

4- Eternity belongs exclusively to Him, and existence is perishable. «زالتا»

5- If it were not for Divine forbearance, the celestial spheres would collapse upon the transgressors. «یمسك السموات - كان حلیماً غفوراً» (When the Resurrection occurs, the sun, moon, stars, and earth will be transformed and scattered. Therefore, if their collapse is prevented in this world, it is due to His forbearance.)

6- The God Who maintains existence through His power, if He grants respite to the disbelievers, it is due to forbearance, not inability. «كان حلیماً»

Ayah

وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ لَئِن جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ لَّيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْ إِحْدَى ٱلْأُمَمِ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ مَّا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا (٤۲)
They had sworn by Allah with solemn oaths that if a warner were to come to them, they would be better guided than any of the nations. But when a warner came to them, it only increased their distance [from the truth], (42)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries (tafasir): When the polytheists heard that whenever a prophet came to the Jews, they showed obstinacy; they swore an oath and said: We are not like that. If a prophet comes to us, we will accept his submission without obstinacy, and we will be guided sooner and better than other nations. But as soon as the Prophet came to them, they showed nothing but aversion. May Allah forbid that those who are awaiting Hadrat Mahdi (عجّل اللّه تعالى فرجه الشریف) and say: If He comes, we will do such and such, should take a stand against Him when He arrives.

Messages

1- The polytheists accepted Allah, held Him sacred, and swore oaths by Him. «اقسموا باللّه»

2- Not every oath can be trusted. «اقسموا باللّه - فلمّا جاءهم...»

3- Deeds matter, not mere rhetoric. Claims are many, but actions are few. «اقسموا - الاّ نفورا»

4- For the guidance of people, both good tidings and warning are necessary, but for a deviated society, warning is more important. (The word "nadhīr" [نذیر] has appeared twice.)

Ayah

ٱسْتِكْبَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَكْرَ ٱلسَّيِّئِ ۚ وَلَا يَحِيقُ ٱلْمَكْرُ ٱلسَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِۦ ۚ فَهَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا سُنَّتَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ ۚ فَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَحْوِيلًا (٤۳)
due to their domineering [conduct] in the land and their devising of evil schemes; and evil schemes beset only their authors. So do they await anything except the precedent of the ancients? Yet you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent, and you will never find any revision in Allah’s precedent. (43)

Key Points

«تبدیل» (Tabdīl) means replacing one thing with another, such as Tayammum instead of Ghusl. However, «تحویل» (Taḥwīl) means altering the time, place, shape, or form of a thing without substituting it with something else. If a doctor gives a patient syrup, and the patient, instead of drinking it, throws it away and tells the doctor, "I drank it," he imagines that he has tricked the doctor and pulled a fast one on Him, whereas the patient has only deceived himself, not the doctor. «لایحیق المكر السّیى ء الاّ باهله» (The evil plot encompasses none but its own people.)

Messages

1- The source of fleeing and aversion from the path of the Prophets is either arrogance or plotting. (Perhaps the arrogance is from the disbelievers and the plotting is from the hypocrites, or perhaps arrogance itself is the ground for the arrogant person's plotting.) «استكباراً... و مكر السیّى ء»

2- The result of plotting returns to the person himself (the consequence and punishment for arrogance and plotting are not transferred to others). «لا یحیق المكر السّیّى ء الاّ باهله»

Do no evil, lest evil befall you, Dig no pit, lest you fall into it yourself.

3- Allah has established fixed laws and traditions (Sunan) in society that apply to all peoples and nations, such as the tradition of trial, the tradition of aiding the believers, and the tradition of punishing plotters and the arrogant. Indeed, the happiness or misery, and the honor or downfall of a society, depend on following or abandoning these Divine traditions. «فلن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلاً... تحویلاً»

4- History possesses a fixed system and law. (By looking at one part of history, one can deduce the law governing another part of history.) «فلن تجد لسنّة اللّه تبدیلاً»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَكَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ۚ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُۥ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَلِيمًا قَدِيرًا (٤٤)
Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more powerful than them, and Allah is not to be frustrated by anything in the heavens or on the earth. Indeed, He is all-knowing, all-powerful. (44)

Key Points

Following the preceding Ayah, this verse serves as an explanation for becoming acquainted with the Divine traditions (Sunan) throughout history, and for understanding the outcome and end of arrogance (Istikbar).

Messages

1- Islam advocates travel and journeying for gaining experience. «أوَلم یسیروا»

2- Travel and journeying must be purposeful. «یسیروا فینظروا»

3- The history of predecessors is among the sources for recognizing the traditions (Sunan) governing societies. (The past is a lamp for the future.) «كیف كان عاقبة الّذین من قبلهم»

4- Visiting the ancient ruins of civilizations is recommended. «أولم یسیروا...»

5- Do not pay attention to the false displays of the arrogant; look at their ultimate end. «عاقبة الّذین كانوا اشدّ منهم قوّة»

6- Do not be arrogant about your own power, for people stronger than you have been uprooted and destroyed. «كانوا اشدّ منهم قوّة»

7- Nothing can overcome the will and power of Allah. «و ما كان اللّه لیعجزه»

8- Allah both knows the arrogant and has the power to deal with them. «علیماً قدیراً»

Ayah

وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّاسَ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ مَا تَرَكَ عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِهَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ بَصِيرًۢا (٤٥)
Were Allah to take humans to task because of what they have earned, He would not leave any living being on its back. But He respites them until a specified time, and when their time comes, [He retributes them in accordance with their works], for Allah has been watching His servants. (45)

Key Points

| Imam Sajjad عليه السلام says in his supplication (Munajat): "كان جزائى فى اول ما عصیتك النار" (587) O Allah, my recompense for the first sin I committed was the Fire, and whatever time I have remained alive is due to Your grace and respite towards me so that perhaps I may repent.

(587) Sahifa Sajjadiyya, Supplication 16.

Messages

1- If the decree is issued to arrest the intoxicated, everyone in the city will be arrested. "و لو یؤاخذ اللّه الناس... ماترك... من دابّة"

2- A sin that carries punishment is one committed knowingly and intentionally. "یؤاخذ... بما كسبوا"

3- The philosophy behind delaying the punishment of people from this world until the Day of Judgment is the survival of the human race and the continuation of life on Earth. If people were destroyed immediately due to sin, no human would remain, and the species would become extinct. "ما ترك على ظهرها من دابّة"

4- Allah is patient, forgiving, merciful, and grants respite; He does not immediately punish people in this world for their bad deeds. "یؤخّرهم الى أجل مسمّىً"

5- Deaths, opportunities, and periods of respite are according to the Divine Will and possess order, calculation, and measure. "یؤخّرهم الى أجل مسمّىً"

6- The duration of human life is predetermined. "اجلٍ مُسمّى"

7- Allah's granting of respite to sinful servants and the delay of their punishment until the Day of Judgment is among the Divine traditions and programs. "یؤخّرهم الى أجل مسمّىً"

8- Allah's granting of respite to sinners is an act based on knowledge. "بعباده بصیراً"

(Tens of millions of people have been granted the success of repentance years after committing sins and have corrected their wrongdoing. If Allah had punished them immediately, they all would have become inhabitants of the Fire.)
